UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile information within the LifeOS application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity. This typically corresponds to the Firebase user ID (UID).

displayName.description: The user's chosen display name or full name.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was initially created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Income.title: Income
Income.description: Represents an individual income entry recorded by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Income entry.

date.description: The date when the income was received.

amount.description: The numerical amount of income received.

source.description: The source or origin of the income (e.g., Salary, Freelance, Gift).

description.description: Optional detailed description or notes about the income.

Expense.title: Expense
Expense.description: Represents an individual expense entry recorded by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Expense entry.

date.description: The date when the expense occurred.

amount.description: The numerical amount of the expense.

notes.description: Optional detailed notes or description about the expense.

Bill.title: Bill
Bill.description: Represents a recurring or one-time bill a user needs to track and pay.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Bill entity.

name.description: The name or description of the bill (e.g., 'Rent', 'Electricity', 'Internet').

amountDue.description: The amount of money due for this bill.

dueDate.description: The date by which the bill is due.

isPaid.description: Indicates whether the bill has been paid.

recurrence.description: The frequency at which the bill recurs.

notes.description: Optional additional notes or details about the bill.

Purchase.title: Purchase
Purchase.description: Represents an individual item purchased by the user, often part of a shopping list or expense tracking.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Purchase entity.

itemName.description: The name of the item purchased.

purchaseDate.description: The date and time the item was purchased.

store.description: The store or vendor where the item was purchased.

isEssential.description: Indicates whether the purchase is considered essential (e.g., for budgeting).

notes.description: Optional additional notes about the purchase.

CreditCard.title: CreditCard
CreditCard.description: Stores encrypted credit card details for financial tracking within the secure vault, assuming client-side encryption.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CreditCard entity.

cardName.description: A user-friendly name for the credit card (e.g., 'My Visa Card', 'Amex Gold').

lastFourDigits.description: The last four digits of the credit card number, used for identification without revealing the full number.

encryptedCardNumber.description: The full credit card number, encrypted using AES-256 client-side before storage.

encryptedExpiryDate.description: The card's expiry date (MM/YY), encrypted using AES-256 client-side before storage.

encryptedCvv.description: The Card Verification Value (CVV), encrypted using AES-256 client-side before storage.

cardHolderName.description: The name of the cardholder as it appears on the card.

bankName.description: The name of the bank or financial institution that issued the card.

cardType.description: The type of credit card (e.g., Visa, Mastercard, American Express).

notes.description: Optional additional notes about the credit card.

definition.description: Stores individual user profile documents. Typically, there is one document per user with a fixed ID like 'details', and the UserProfile.id field within the document matches the userId from the path. Includes denormalized 'userId' field for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (Firebase Authentication UID).

params.name: profileId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user's profile document. Often a fixed string like 'details'.

definition.description: Stores individual income entries for a specific user. Each document contains a 'userId' field that matches the path's userId for authorization independence and ownership enforcement.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (Firebase Authentication UID).

params.name: incomeId
params.description: The unique identifier for an income entry.

definition.description: Stores individual expense entries for a specific user. Each document contains a 'userId' field that matches the path's userId for authorization independence and ownership enforcement. Supports categories like gas, grocery, eating out, and misc.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (Firebase Authentication UID).

params.name: expenseId
params.description: The unique identifier for an expense entry.

definition.description: Stores individual bill entries for a specific user. Each document contains a 'userId' field that matches the path's userId for authorization independence and ownership enforcement.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (Firebase Authentication UID).

params.name: billId
params.description: The unique identifier for a bill entry.

definition.description: Stores individual purchase entries for a specific user. Each document contains a 'userId' field that matches the path's userId for authorization independence and ownership enforcement.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (Firebase Authentication UID).

params.name: purchaseId
params.description: The unique identifier for a purchase entry.

definition.description: Stores encrypted credit card details for a specific user. Each document contains a 'userId' field that matches the path's userId for authorization independence and ownership enforcement.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (Firebase Authentication UID).

params.name: creditCardId
params.description: The unique identifier for a credit card entry.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for LifeOS prioritizes strong authorization independence and clear, debuggable security rules by following a strict path-based ownership model. All user-specific data is organized under a top-level `/users/{userId}` path, with each distinct data type stored in its own subcollection. For instance, user profiles are at `/users/{userId}/profile/{profileId}`, income entries at `/users/{userId}/income/{incomeId}`, and so on for expenses, bills, purchases, and credit cards. Each document within these subcollections explicitly includes a `userId` field that matches the `userId` in its path (e.g., `Income.userId == {userId}`). This denormalization is crucial for authorization independence, as it allows security rules to check `request.auth.uid == userId` directly against the document's path or its internal `userId` field without needing expensive `get()` calls to a parent document. This design ensures that atomic operations (like transactions or batched writes) remain performant and secure, as authorization context is self-contained within each document.

This structure also inherently supports Querying for Authorized Data (QAPs) by leveraging the pre-existing Firestore security rules: `match /users/{userId}/{document=**} { allow read, write: if request.auth != null && request.auth.uid == userId; }`. Because all of a user's data is partitioned under their unique `userId` in the path, any `list` operation performed by an authenticated user will automatically be restricted to only return documents owned by that user. For example, a query to `/users/currentUserId/income` will only retrieve the current user's income records, and the security rules will prevent access to any other user's data. This eliminates the need for complex, filter-based security rules which are prone to errors and performance issues. Each collection (e.g., `income`, `expenses`) maintains a homogeneous security posture, meaning all documents within it have the same access requirements (owned by the `userId` in the path), further simplifying rule logic. The consistent naming conventions for paths and parameters (`{userId}`, `{incomeId}`) enhance predictability and debuggability.
